revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tsurusaki_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''鶴崎神社'''（つるさきじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[都窪郡]][[早島町]]にある神社。
== 歴史 == *[[1350年]]（[[貞和]]6年）、[[吉備津神社]]から[[大吉備津彦命]]荒魂を勧請し創建。
== 社宝 == *[[上野]][[寛永寺]]絵馬（1716年、町指定重要文化財） *早島十景扁額（1890年、町指定重要文化財） *東参道常夜灯（1856年、町指定重要文化財）
== 参考資料 == *『吉備の国寺社巡り』山陽新聞社（2011年）
== 周辺施設 == *[[花ござ手織り伝承館]]
== 関連項目 == *[[吉備津神社]] *[[吉備津彦神社]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:つるさきしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Tsurusaki Shrine |image = {{画像募集中|cat=都窪郡}} |address = 2220 Hayashima, Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|37|N|133|52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (荒魂) |status = |establishment = 1350 (Jōwa 6) |architecture = |annual_festival = |rituals = }}
'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * **Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno** (1716) * **Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views** (1890) * **Stone Lanterns on the East Approach** (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * **Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum**
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website]
== See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurusaki Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Hayashima Town, Okayama]] [[Category:Historical Sites in Okayama]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurusaki Shrine |image = {{image募集中|cat=都窪郡}} |location = 2220 Hayashima, Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|37|N|133|52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (荒魂) |status = |establishment = 1350 (Jōwa 6) |architecture = |annual_festival = |rituals = }}
'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * **Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno** (1716) * **Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views** (1890) * **Stone Lanterns on the East Approach** (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * **Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum**
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website]
== See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website]
== See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website]

== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''

'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also! == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurusaki Shrine |image = {{image requested|cat=都窪郡}} |location = 2220 Hayashima, Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|37|N|133|52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (荒魂) |status = |establishment = 1350 (Jōwa 6) |architecture = |annual_festival = |rituals = }}
'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurusaki Shrine |image = {{image requested|cat=都窪郡}} |location = 2220 Hayashima, Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|37|N|133|52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (荒魂) |status = |establishment = 1350 (Jōwa 6) |architecture = |annual_festival = |rituals = }}
'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]
{{Shinto Horizontal}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 鶴崎神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=都窪郡}} |所在地= [[岡山県]][[都窪郡]][[早島町]]早島2220 |位置= |祭神= [[大吉備津彦命]] |社格= |創建= [[1350年]]（[[貞和]]6年） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''鶴崎神社'''（つるさきじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[都窪郡]][[早島町]]にある神社。
== 歴史 == *[[1350年]]（[[貞和]]6年）、[[吉備津神社]]から[[大吉備津彦命]]荒魂を勧請し創建。
== 社宝 == *[[上野]][[寛永寺]]絵馬（1716年、町指定重要文化財） *早島十景扁額（1890年、町指定重要文化財） *東参道常夜灯（1856年、町指定重要文化財）
== 参考資料 == *『吉備の国寺社巡り』山陽新聞社（2011年） 鶴崎神社ホームページ http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/
== 周辺施設 == *[[花ござ手織り伝承館]]
== 関連項目 == *[[吉備津神社]] *[[吉備津彦神社]] {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:つるさきしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:早島町の建築物]] [[Category:早島町の歴史]]

== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).
== History == Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].
== Cultural Properties == The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties: * '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716) * '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890) * '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)
== Nearby Facilities == * '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''
== References == * ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011) * [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website] == See Also == * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] * [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]
{{Shinto Horizontal}}
